mnemonics: DECOMPOSE: Decomposition, ETS, Components, Outliers, Model, Period, Out-of-sample, Seasonality, Errors
keyPoints: Seasonal decomposition separates a time series into trend, seasonal, and irregular components.
The seasonal component is a pattern that repeats at fixed, known intervals (e.g., every 12 months).
Models like Seasonal ARIMA (SARIMA) and Exponential Smoothing (ETS) are specifically designed to handle and forecast seasonal data.
commonMistakes: Failing to account for seasonality before analyzing the trend, leading to misleading conclusions.
Confusing a cyclic pattern (no fixed period) with a seasonal pattern (fixed period).
Using a non-seasonal model on seasonal data, which will produce poor forecasts.
